text: Digital Signatures @i © e ¢ Verifies INTEGRITY (WHAT) & AUTHENTICITY (WHO) ¢ HASH of the data is taken, original data remains unaltered (integrity) ¢ This allows normal use without worrying about hashing/keys e Digitally sign the HASH (using private key). Authenticates the hash. e Public key can be widely distributed and trusted ¢ .. hash cannot be changed as nobody else has the private key. ¢ Document cannot be changed as this invalidates the HASH. e Trust public key => trust private key => trust entity => trust data
